snippet: Jobs, Skills and Pathways Coordination program funding assists schools with the coordination and delivery of vocational and applied learning programs, in particular, with Vocational Education and Training Delivered to School Students (VDSS), work-related learning, and the VCE Vocational Major and Victorian Pathways Certificate. The key objectives of Jobs, Skills and Pathways Coordination funding are to: For further information, refer to the department’s policy on Jobs, Skills and Pathways Coordination . In 2026, the following schools are eligible to receive funding for Jobs, Skills and Pathways Coordination funding: Each eligible school will receive a flat amount equivalent to 0.3 FTE of an Education Support Staff salary as at 31 December 2025 (Level 1, Range 4-6) including superannuation and payroll tax. Funding is calculated at the Confirmed Cycle and is allocated through SRP cash funding on a termly basis.
